items.blurb: Celtics Rout 76ers 123-91 In Game 1, Jayson Tatum Posts 25-Point Double-Double — The Boston Celtics opened their first round series with a commanding 123-91 victory over the Philadelphia 76ers on Sunday. items.title: Jayson Tatum leads Celtics to rout of 76ers in series opener
items.blurb: Jayson Tatum leads Celtics to rout of 76ers in series opener — Jayson Tatum had 25 points, 11 rebounds and seven assists in his first playoff game since rupturing his right Achilles tendon last season, and the Boston Celtics rolled past the Philadelphia 76ers 123-91 in Game 1 of their Eastern Conference first-round matchup on Sunday.

items.title: James Harden Notched Historic Career Feat On Monday
items.blurb: James Harden Notched Historic Career Feat On Monday —   Los Angeles Clippers guard James Harden entered Monday’s matchup against the Philadelphia 76ers needing just one point to reach a significant career milestone. The Clippers fell to Philadelphia in a closely contested game at the Wells Fargo Center on Tuesday, but Harden delivered 28 points along with six rebounds and five assists.

items.title: Devin Booker Signs Two-Year, $145 Million Max Contract with Suns
items.blurb: Devin Booker Signs Two-Year, $145 Million Max Contract with Suns — On July 10, 2025, Devin Booker made NBA history by signing a two-year, $145 million max extension with the Phoenix Suns. This deal, the richest in annual salary in league history, solidifies Booker’s position as the face of the franchise and sends a clear message about the Suns’ direction for the rest of the decade.
